_comment: Eval set for aviation-spi-spt-framework (06-06 hse-aviation). Intake-gate + every-SPI-has-threshold-and-owner + no-invented-figure + the de-id CLEAN fixture case. The seeded deid-leak.md (auto-discovered by test_sector_deid_pairs) proves the de-id hard-fail.

evals.query: Set up some safety KPIs for us.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses to draft until the named operator and the hazards/objectives the SPIs must track are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake before designing any SPI.
Refuses a generic KPI list — elicits the named operator and the specific hazards/objectives each SPI must map to (specificity).
Captures whether each indicator is leading or lagging and any baseline data.

evals.query: Design SPIs for our unstabilised-approach and runway-excursion hazards.
evals.expected_behavior: Designs SPIs each with a defined alert level and target level, mapped to the owning hazard, and never invents a performance figure.
expectations: Every SPI carries a defined alert level AND a target level (SPT) — flags any SPI without a threshold.
Every SPI maps to an owning hazard or safety objective — flags any orphan SPI.
Uses incident_rates / KB-DATA-TRIR-BENCHMARKS (source+year) for trend context and records any absent figure as [GAP] — does NOT fabricate a rate.

evals.query: Build the SPI framework from this occurrence dataset.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; any named crew/reporter in the occurrence data is pseudonymised to a role label; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are listed up front.
Named crew/reporter and contact become role labels; no re-identification key is embedded.
No real name, contact, address or reporter identity in the circulated framework (de_identification hard-fail on any leak).
